title: Ex-Offender Reentry Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Ex-Offender Reentry Programs 11 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Women Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTRAL CITY CONCERN IMANI CENTER HOUSING RAPID RESPONSE INFORMATIONAL RECORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Responds to the needs of houseless ex-offenders through safe housing programs, medical and mental care, addiction treatment, and other vital supports. 2020 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Houseless individuals age 18 and over Must be referred by parole officer or other re-entry program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 226-4060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE CLACKAMAS COUNTY TRANSITION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to care and case management services providing assistance with such services as SNAP enrollment, 12 step meetings, mental health and substance use assessments, cognitive therapy, finding employment and/or housing, parenting class referrals, GED courses, and hygiene items. Also offers on-site distribution and training in usage of Naloxone. 2219 Kaen Road Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults with involvement in the criminal justice system Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 722-6199 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FREE ON THE OUTSIDE FREE ON THE OUTSIDE HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing and sober living facilities. Clients may not use illegal drugs or alcohol during their stay. 529 Molalla Avenue Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Anyone experiencing housing instability. Does not turn people away for their criminal history or credit. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 383-1834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GUIDANCE 2 GREATNESS BEYOND REENTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with criminal record and eviction expungement, job development, workplace training, financial literacy, felony rights, and reentry training. May be able to provide program participants with supplies such as personal protective equipment (PPE) and a desktop computer. Eligibility: -Age 18 and older -Reentry Training for women ages 16-24 -Focus on the African American and BIPOC community Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH WORKS JOB CONNECTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides job training, individualized career and life coaching, personal finance and life skills education, job placement, and work experience opportunities. Works with the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) program. Also provides job placement and support for ex-offenders in addition to a welfare to work program. 1930 S 6th Street Klamath Falls, OR 976013370 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 887-8495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MEDICINE WHEEL RECOVERY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient services including alcohol and drug treatment, co-occurring disorder care, DUI programming, gambling treatment, mental health counseling, and cultural activities, with cultural and spiritual wellness guiding all service planning. Services include assessments, individual and group therapy, and crisis and safety planning, with referrals to community partners for medication management or specialized mental health needs. Works with community agencies to support participants involved in courts and social service systems, helping with re-entry and reducing barriers related to housing, employment, education, stigma, and rebuilding trust. Offers recidivism risk assessments, Rent Well classes, life-skills and employment readiness groups, parenting support, resource navigation, and volunteer opportunities. 58147 Columbia River Highway Suite C Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Age 18 and older. Provides culturally specific services for the Native American community. Some services are gender-specific, while other services are co-ed. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm DUII treatment: Monday-Friday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 396-5322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION REENTRY SERVICES EX-OFFENDER RESOURCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A resource center supporting individuals transitioning from incarceration, those on probation or parole, and others affected by the criminal justice system. Offers weekly legal clinics for civil matters, help with health insurance applications, and classes on parenting, personal finance, tenant readiness, and motivation. Provides consultations, information, and referrals for people and families impacted by incarceration. A computer lab is available for tutoring, GED preparation, job searches, and other needs. 2085 Commercial Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Current or past involvement with criminal justice system. Preference given to those currently on parole supervision and recently released from incarceration. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 332-5332 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC ROOSEVELT CROSSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and supportive services to individuals released in Lane County from prison or jail, and currently on correctional supervision in Lane County. Average length of program is 60-90 days. Services in addition to housing include: case management; behavioral health; drug and alcohol treatment; as well as assistance with clothing, ID, bus passes/bicycles, employment search, and finding long-term housing. Specialized services available for veterans. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Male-identifying individuals on correctional supervision in or releasing to Lane County. -Priority for transitional housing provided to those releasing from state prison who would otherwise be homeless and who are at greatest risk to become reengaged with the criminal legal system without supports. -Participants expected to follow program rules, remain clean and sober (tested by urinalysis and breathalyzer), obtain full-time employment or be enrolled in school full-time, and follow all conditions of their supervision. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 485-8341 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC ROSS SHEPARD REENTRY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment, housing and resource support to individuals with conviction histories. Services include job counseling, interview preparation support, and educational workshops on resume writing, saving and budgeting, and job/housing search strategies. Also provides assistance preparing educational and financial aid applications, housing packets/applications, and other supports to help individuals with conviction histories overcome barriers to employment and housing access. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals with conviction histories Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 505-5682 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC DOWNTOWN CAMPUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and supportive services for individuals released in Lane County from Oregon State Prison or the local jail who are currently on correctional supervision. The average length of the program is 90 to 120 days. Services include case management, behavioral health support, drug and alcohol treatment, and assistance with clothing, identification, bus passes or bicycles, employment search, and finding long-term housing. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female, released from Oregon State Prison or Lane County Jail, and under supervision in Lane County. Participants must follow program rules, remain clean and sober (verified by urinalysis and breathalyzer testing), obtain full-time employment or enroll in school full-time, and comply with all conditions of their supervision. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 485-8341 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM RESTORATION HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing services. Guests will have access to case management, job readiness training and placement assistance, life skills classes, relapse prevention, mental health supports, and faith-based guidance. Individuals who have completed the program may be referred to long term rehousing re-entry services. 650 Locust Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older with any criminal history Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 363-2805 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON VOA OREGON OUTPATIENT SERVICES HOME BASE RECOVERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clean and sober housing and addiction treatment. Participants in the program agree to attend treatment while being provided with monitored housing, recovery mentors, and addiction and mental health counseling. 10564 SE Washington Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Must be in recovery from alcohol or drug addiction Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 227-6092 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WELCOME HOME OREGON INC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides re-entry housing along with assistance for food, clothing, employment, and mentorship. 510 NW 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Individuals who were recently released from a correctional facility and are on supervised release Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment only Email (541) 474-5178 Get Directions More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN HOUSING RAPID RESPONSE INFORMATIONAL RECORD CLACKAMAS COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE CLACKAMAS COUNTY TRANSITION CENTER FREE ON THE OUTSIDE FREE ON THE OUTSIDE HOUSING KLAMATH WORKS JOB CONNECTION MEDICINE WHEEL RECOVERY SERVICES MEDICINE WHEEL RECOVERY SERVICES MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY EX-OFFENDER RESOURCE PROGRAM SPONSORS INC ROOSEVELT CROSSING UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM RESTORATION HOUSE VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON HOME BASE RECOVERY WELCOME HOME OREGON INC WELCOME HOME OREGON INC
